# Vectronomy

> Professional-grade browser-based XCS to SVG converter and vector editing studio for laser cutting, CNC, and precision fabrication.

## About

Vectronomy is a free, open-source web application that runs entirely in your browser. It converts XCS project files (used by xTool laser cutters) into clean, production-ready SVG files. Beyond conversion, it provides a full vector editing studio with professional-grade tools.

## Key Features

- **XCS to SVG Conversion**: Drag-and-drop .xcs project files and instantly convert them to standards-compliant SVG
- **Vector Drawing Tools**: Bezier pen, pencil, line, polyline, rectangle, circle, polygon, star, and spiral tools
- **Node Editing**: High-precision anchor point and handle editing for full path control
- **Pathfinder Operations**: Unite, subtract, intersect, exclude, and divide boolean operations
- **Layer Management**: Multi-layer workspace with grouping, locking, and z-order control
- **Align & Distribute**: Full alignment and distribution grid for precise layout
- **Image Tracing**: Silhouette, centerline, edge detection, and posterized multi-color tracing
- **CNC/Fabrication Tools**: Kerf offset, pocket hatching, chamfering, drilling, milling tabs, concentric hatching
- **Export Options**: SVG, PNG, JPEG, source text, and clipboard export
- **Grid & Snap**: Configurable grid overlay with snap-to-grid support
- **Rulers**: Interactive rulers with real-world unit display (mm/inch)
- **Undo/Redo**: Full undo/redo history with drawing-level granularity
- **Dark/Light Theme**: System-aware theme switching
- **No Account Required**: Everything runs locally in the browser — no sign-up, no data sent to servers

## Pages

- `/` — Main Studio (vector editor and XCS converter)
- `/roadmap` — Product roadmap with 140 planned features across 10 divisions
- `/help` — Interactive help center with tutorials and keyboard shortcut reference

## Technical Details

- Built with TypeScript and Vite
- Client-side only — no server-side processing
- Works in all modern browsers with HTML5, Canvas, and SVG support
- MIT Licensed

## Links

- Website: https://vectronomy.vercel.app
- GitHub: https://github.com/corned-dev/vectronomy
